explanation: boundscheck(False) removes index validation → significant speedup in loops (use carefully).

question_text: What does typing.Concatenate do when used with ParamSpec?
option_a: Adds extra positional parameters to a callable signature
explanation: Concatenate[ParamSpec, int, str] adds extra args to the preserved signature.

question_text: Which asyncio synchronization primitive supports both wait/notify and timeout?
explanation: Condition supports wait/notify and can be used with timeouts via wait_for.

question_text: What is the most precise ABC to type-hint something that supports membership testing (in operator)?
explanation: collections.abc.Container requires __contains__ (supports 'in').

question_text: Which mypy flag makes it treat missing return type annotations as errors?
explanation: --disallow-untyped-defs errors on functions without any return type annotation.

question_text: Which method should you override to customize what happens when an object is pickled?
explanation: __reduce__, __getstate__, __setstate__ all participate in pickling customization.

question_text: What is the time complexity of heapq.heappushpop(heap
explanation: A
topic: heappushpop combines push + pop in one operation — O(log n) total.

question_text: Which asyncio construct (3.11+) groups tasks and cancels them together on exception or exit?
explanation: TaskGroup automatically cancels children when exiting or on exception.

question_text: Which metaclass special method lets you return a different type instead of the class being created?
explanation: __new__ in a metaclass can return any object instead of a new class instance.

question_text: What is the correct way to implement a descriptor that auto-converts values on assignment?
explanation: A data descriptor needs both __get__ and __set__ to control read/write behavior.

question_text: Which decorator pattern is used when you want to wrap a function without changing its signature?
explanation: @functools.wraps is the standard way to preserve name, docstring, and signature.

question_text: Which asyncio method schedules a callback to run as soon as possible in the next iteration?
explanation: call_soon() adds a callback to the run queue for the next event loop iteration.

question_text: What is the main reason __slots__ can break when used with multiple inheritance?
option_b: Subclasses must redeclare all parent slots
explanation: __slots__ are not automatically inherited — subclasses need their own slots or layout conflicts can arise.

question_text: Which gc function can be used to manually trigger collection of a specific generation?
explanation: gc.collect(generation=0) collects only generation 0 (young objects).

question_text: In Cython
option_a: what does cdef inline do for a function?
explanation: A
topic: cdef inline requests the C compiler to inline the function for speed.

question_text: Which collections class provides a dict-like object that calls a factory for missing keys?
explanation: defaultdict automatically calls the factory function for missing keys.

question_text: Which concurrency pattern should be used when you need to share a large numpy array between processes with zero-copy?
explanation: shared_memory allows direct access to the same memory buffer across processes.

question_text: What does mypy --check-untyped-defs do?
option_a: Checks bodies of functions without type annotations
explanation: --check-untyped-defs enables type checking inside functions that lack annotations.

question_text: Which method should be overridden to customize the behavior of bytes(obj)?
explanation: __bytes__ returns a bytes object representation of the instance.

question_text: What is the most reliable way to cancel a group of related asyncio tasks?
explanation: TaskGroup automatically cancels children on exit or exception (Python 3.11+).

question_text: Which weakref container is ideal for tracking a set of live objects?
explanation: WeakSet automatically removes objects when they are garbage collected.

question_text: What is the cleanest way to implement a class property that is computed once and cached?
explanation: @cached_property (3.8+) is the recommended modern idiom for lazy class-level properties.

question_text: Which Cython feature allows declaring C-level variables inside Python functions?
explanation: cdef declares C-typed variables that exist only at C speed inside the function.

question_text: What does typing.Literal allow in type hints?
option_a: Restricting a value to specific literal values
explanation: Literal[foo"
mdn_link: True] restricts the type to exactly those values."

question_text: Which asyncio synchronization primitive can be awaited multiple times without blocking?
explanation: Event can be awaited by many tasks — all are released when set().

question_text: Which collections.abc class is appropriate for type-hinting read-only mappings?
explanation: Mapping is the ABC for immutable dict-like objects (read-only view).

question_text: Which mypy flag is used to treat missing imports as silent (no error)?
explanation: --ignore-missing-imports suppresses errors for missing third-party stubs.

question_text: Which special method customizes behavior when an object is used as a format specifier?
explanation: __format__(self, format_spec) controls how format(obj, spec) and f-strings behave.

question_text: What is the time complexity of heapq.heapreplace(heap
explanation: A
topic: heapreplace pops the smallest and pushes the new item — O(log n) total.

question_text: Which asyncio feature allows running synchronous iterators asynchronously?
explanation: There is no direct built-in; you usually wrap in run_in_executor or to_thread.

question_text: What is the main advantage of using collections.abc.ByteString over bytes?
option_a: It supports both bytes and bytearray
explanation: ByteString is the ABC for byte-like objects (bytes, bytearray, memoryview, custom types).

question_text: Which metaclass method is invoked when a subclass is created to allow runtime validation?
explanation: __init_subclass__ is called automatically on the base class when any subclass is defined — ideal for validation or registration.

question_text: What is the correct way to make a descriptor read-only from the instance side?
explanation: Omitting __set__ (non-data descriptor) or raising in __set__ both make the attribute effectively read-only.

question_text: Which decorator is specifically designed for creating cached class properties in Python 3.8+?
explanation: @cached_property is the cleanest way to cache expensive instance properties.

question_text: Which asyncio function allows awaiting multiple coroutines and returns the first one to complete?
explanation: asyncio.first_completed()
mdn_link: asyncio.wait() with FIRST_COMPLETED returns when the first task finishes.

question_text: What is the primary benefit of using __slots__ = ('__weakref__'
option_b: Enables weak references while keeping memory savings
explanation: A
topic: Including '__weakref__' in __slots__ allows weakref support without restoring the full __dict__.

question_text: Which gc module constant enables printing of all unreachable objects that could not be collected?
explanation: DEBUG_LEAK prints objects involved in reference cycles that prevent collection.

question_text: In Cython
option_a: what does the directive @cython.boundscheck(False) mainly improve?
option_b: Speed of array indexing in loops
explanation: A
topic: Disabling bounds checking removes safety overhead — very effective in tight numerical loops.

question_text: Which collections class provides a dictionary that remembers insertion order?
explanation: OrderedDict maintains insertion order (though dict does too since 3.7, OrderedDict has extra methods).

question_text: Which concurrency module provides a thread-local storage object?
explanation: threading.local() creates thread-local variables — each thread gets its own copy.

question_text: What does mypy --warn-redundant-casts do?
option_a: Warns when a cast is unnecessary
explanation: --warn-redundant-casts reports when cast() is used but the type is already known.

question_text: Which method customizes behavior when an object is used with the format() function or f-strings?
explanation: __format__(self, format_spec) controls formatting behavior in f-strings and format().

question_text: Which asyncio construct provides a context manager for applying a timeout to a block of awaits?
explanation: asyncio.timeout(delay) is the modern context manager for timeouts (Python 3.11+).

question_text: Which weakref container automatically removes entries when values become unreachable?
explanation: WeakValueDictionary drops items when the value object is garbage collected.

question_text: What is the recommended way to create a singleton that works with inheritance?
explanation: A metaclass with overridden __call__ is the most robust and inheritance-friendly singleton pattern.

question_text: In Cython
option_a: which keyword declares a variable or function that exists only at C speed?
explanation: A
topic: cdef declares C-level entities (fast, no Python overhead).

question_text: What does typing.TypeAlias allow you to do in modern Python?
option_a: Define a new name for a complex type
explanation: TypeAlias creates a readable name for complex types (checked statically).

question_text: Which asyncio primitive is best for implementing a barrier (wait for N tasks to reach a point)?
explanation: asyncio.Event + manual counter is the standard pattern for barriers.

question_text: Which collections.abc class should be used to type-hint objects that support iteration and length?
explanation: A
topic: Collection combines Sized, Iterable, and Container — good for general collections.

question_text: Which mypy flag reports unused type ignore comments?
explanation: --warn-unused-ignores warns when # type: ignore suppresses nothing.

question_text: Which special method customizes behavior when an object is used in bool(obj)?
explanation: __bool__ returns True/False for truth value testing (replaces __nonzero__ in Python 3).

question_text: What is the time complexity of heapq.merge(*iterables) when merging k sorted iterables of total size n?
explanation: merge uses a min-heap of size k → O(n log k) overall.

question_text: Which asyncio feature introduced in Python 3.11 simplifies structured concurrency?
explanation: TaskGroup provides structured concurrency with automatic cancellation and exception handling.
